title: F1 2026 Engines Explained in Under 3 Minutes
description:
F1 2026 is here… and it’s not what you think.

With the removal of the MGU-H, a 50/50 power split, and fully sustainable fuel, Formula 1 has entered a completely new era. But while the cars are faster in some areas, there’s a hidden problem: energy management.

In this video, we break down:
– Why turbo lag is back  
– What “super-clipping” really means  
– How energy deployment is changing racing  
– And why F1 is now more about software than ever 

 Check out our website for more F1 content: ht

title: Doriane Pin: F1 Academy Champion, Still Pushing | F1 Beyond The Grid Podcast
description:
From the first time she sat in a kart, aged 9, Doriane Pin has wanted to race in Formula 1. F1 Academy champion, Mercedes Development Driver, poised to drive an F1 car for the first time - Doriane is getting closer and closer to that dream.

Doriane tells Tom Clarkson her racing story so far, from feeling like a 'superhero' in her kart, to seeking sponsors at the age of 11, taking on the Le Mans 24 hours, and being spotted by the Mercedes Junior Programme.

title: How Jacques Villeneuve Shook Up F1 | F1 Beyond The Grid Podcast
description:
The son of a motorsport legend, a racer since childhood, Formula 1 was Jacques Villeneuve's destiny. In 1996, the young Canadian arrived at his first Grand Prix and immediately proved he belonged there by taking pole position.

In his rookie season Villeneuve won races, went wheel-to-wheel with Michael Schumacher and took Williams teammate Damon Hill to a final race title-decider. 30 years on, he sits down with Tom Clarkson to remember how he shook up the sport.

title: 2026 F1 Chinese GP race analysis by Peter Windsor
description: New regs or not - McLaren-Mercedes in the race (or not): nothing could detract from the enormity of this day in Shanghai - from the first GP win for Mercedes' Kimi Antonelli. From the pole, he seized the race lead from Ferrari's Sir Lewis Hamilton on lap two - and was thereafter never headed.  Lewis re-applied pressure after the pit stops; and team-mate, George Russell, was up their in P2, pushing hard, as the race unfolded.  Kimi, though, was unperturbed. title: Can Formula 1 Return to India with Adani Group? | F1 Comeback Explained
description:
Formula 1 once made history in India with the Indian Grand Prix at Buddh International Circuit in Greater Noida (2011–2013). But due to tax issues and rising costs, the race disappeared from the F1 calendar.

Now, there are reports that Adani Group is exploring the idea of bringing Formula 1 back to India. This discussion comes as the group is bidding to acquire Jaiprakash Associates Ltd, which owns the Buddh International Circuit.

title: Formula 1 Cars, Explained with Max Verstappen
description:
These are more than cars. They’re science experiments.
If you enjoy this episode, subscribe to support more optimistic stories about tech!

I love Formula 1. This isn’t just a car race, it’s a massive group science competition. Ten teams are all fighting to be first, and on every team there are hundreds of people, spending millions of dollars, all working together to push technology to its limit. 

In this video, we’re gonna show you what it takes to build and drive some of the fastest and most

title: Juan Pablo Montoya's record-breaking lap of Monza | 2004 Italian GP
description:
Colombia's Juan Pablo Montoya averaged an eye-watering 259.83 km/h when setting his pole position lap around Monza back in 2004.

This stood as a record until 2018, when it was narrowly eclipsed by Kimi Raikkonen with an average speed of 263.587km/h: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q5_fo16E4LU

Lewis Hamilton then lowered the record further in 2020, with a lap that averaged 264.363 km/h: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2f1PtJV0vIs

Five years later, Max Verstappen went even faster, with an avera
